title: Session driver: walk a work unit through the state machine as the path of least resistance (FEAT-013 pillar 2 — the predictable half)
description: A driver skill/command (precedent: .claude/skills/run-pi-project-workflows/driver.mjs; the workflow executor's control-plane/subordinate-subprocess architecture) that walks a work unit through the canonical state machine: prompts the current state's required action, dispatches the right agent kind per state (Explore for EXPLORED, general-purpose for IMPLEMENTED, fresh-context for PROBED), runs the npm gates itself, mints phase-state transitions, and refuses out-of-order actions. The driver composes each dispatch prompt deterministically (WASC renderer pattern), inlining the project mandates and the role contracts (stop-on-ambiguity for implementing agents; the machine-parsed output contract for probe/audit agents), since subagents do not receive the UserPromptSubmit injection. Autonomous re-dispatch loops carry a hard iteration cap (then STOP and escalate per mandate-008) and terminate only on deterministic signals (gate exit codes; the parsed findings count). With the phase-state layer it makes the state machine the interface rather than a policy the model recalls — the 'predictable' half of the directive (hooks make deviation fail; the driver makes compliance the path of least resistance). Depends on the full deny surface + state layer. FEAT-013 R17.
acceptance_criteria: The driver walks a sample work unit SURFACED..CLOSED, prompting each state's required action and dispatching the state-appropriate agent kind.
The driver mints phase-state transitions and runs the npm gates itself; an out-of-order action is refused.
The driver performs each state's required dispatch and gate without the operator issuing the underlying commands manually (compliance by construction, observed on the sample walk).
Dispatch prompts are composed deterministically with mandates and role contracts inlined (stop-on-ambiguity; probe output contract); identical inputs produce identical prompts.
Autonomous re-dispatch loops carry a hard iteration cap (then STOP and escalate per mandate-008) and terminate only on deterministic signals (gate exit codes; the parsed findings count).
